articles.title: All the latest updates on AI data centers
articles.summary: Tech companies are rapidly expanding AI data centers as infrastructure foundation, but face global conflicts over power consumption, environmental impact, and community concerns. Disputes range from space-based proposals to legal battles over pollution.

articles.title: PlayStation sees AI as a ‘powerful tool’ to help make games
articles.summary: Sony views AI as a powerful tool for game development while emphasizing that human vision, design, and emotional impact remain central to PlayStation games.

articles.title: Microsoft was worried OpenAI would run off to Amazon and ‘shit-talk’ Azure
articles.summary: Court documents from the Musk v. Altman trial reveal Microsoft executives feared OpenAI would leave for Amazon and criticize Azure. The concerns emerged during early discussions about Microsoft's investment in OpenAI in 2017.

articles.title: Voice AI in India is hard. Wispr Flow is betting on it anyway.
articles.summary: Wispr Flow accelerated growth in India following its Hinglish language rollout, despite ongoing challenges facing voice AI products in the market.

articles.title: So you’ve heard these AI terms and nodded along; let’s fix that
articles.summary: AI's rapid growth has introduced numerous new terms and slang. This glossary defines key words and phrases commonly encountered in AI discussions.

articles.title: Nvidia has already committed $40B to equity AI deals this year
articles.summary: Nvidia has committed $40 billion to equity AI deals in 2024, demonstrating its continued significant investment in the broader AI ecosystem and emerging companies.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway secured $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native cloud infrastructure as an alternative to AWS and Google Cloud. The platform has attracted 2 million developers by addressing limitations of legacy infrastructure for AI applications.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but Goose offers free, open-source alternative with similar functionality running locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment stunt successfully attracted thousands of engineers in a competitive hiring market.

articles.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts learning platform Canvas amid finals
articles.summary: A cyberattack disrupted Canvas learning platform during US finals, forcing schools offline. The platform was restored by Friday after unauthorized network activity exposed usernames, emails, and student IDs.

articles.title: Mozilla says 271 vulnerabilities found by Mythos have "almost no false positives"
articles.summary: Mozilla's AI vulnerability detector Mythos identified 271 Firefox security flaws with minimal false positives. The breakthrough demonstrates AI-assisted vulnerability detection's practical effectiveness in real-world software security.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Terminal windows remain essential tools for computing tasks despite predictions of graphical interfaces replacing command-line interfaces. The command line offers efficiency advantages over point-and-click interfaces for many operations.

articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
articles.summary: In week two of Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, Brockman alleged Musk pushed for profit-seeking structure and sought absolute control. OpenAI claims Musk is suing due to losing control and wanting to undermine a competitor.

articles.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
articles.summary: Eight cruise ship passengers contracted hantavirus, a rare rat-borne virus, with three deaths reported. The ship will dock in the Canary Islands to safely disembark remaining passengers and crew.

articles.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
articles.summary: Society faces uncertainty about AI's widespread integration and societal impact, with concerns about job displacement and economic consequences. There's growing discomfort as AI becomes ubiquitous despite unclear benefits and risks.

articles.title: "OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support"
articles.summary: OncoAgent introduces a dual-tier multi-agent framework for oncology clinical decision support while preserving patient privacy. The system uses AI agents to provide personalized cancer treatment recommendations securely.

articles.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
articles.summary: Researchers introduce EMO, a mixture of experts pretraining method that develops emergent modularity in neural networks. This approach improves model efficiency and specialization capabilities.

articles.title: vLLM V0 to V1: Correctness Before Corrections in RL
articles.summary: vLLM upgrades from V0 to V1, prioritizing correctness in reinforcement learning before applying corrections. The update ensures more reliable model behavior and accuracy.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and its implications. The conversation explores upcoming developments and trends in the artificial intelligence field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling tasks.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, providing farmers and developers with tools to leverage AI and technology for sustainable agricultural practices.

articles.title: See what happens when creative legends use AI to make ads for small businesses.
articles.summary: Google launches The Small Brief, pairing renowned ad industry leaders with local businesses to create AI-powered advertisements, demonstrating how creative professionals can leverage AI tools to support small enterprises.

articles.title: 5 gardening tips you can try right in Search
articles.summary: Google's AI Mode, Search Live, and Shopping features offer five gardening tips to help plants thrive. Users can leverage these tools directly in Search for gardening assistance.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google, XPRIZE, and Range Media Partners launched a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition to support innovative filmmaking exploring AI and technology's future impact.

articles.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
articles.summary: AlphaEvolve, powered by Gemini, uses advanced algorithms to drive impact across business, infrastructure, and scientific fields. The coding agent scales solutions across multiple domains.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery. The initiative explores how AI can assist medical professionals in patient care.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company stole charity status. Evidence revealed includes settlement discussions and allegations that xAI used OpenAI models for training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #243 covers major AI developments including OpenAI's GPT-5.5 with coding improvements, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and discussions on AI safety and potential sabotage concerns.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast #242 discusses OpenAI's new ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text accuracy, Qwen 3.6 Max, and Kimi-K2.6. The episode covers major AI developments from the previous week.

articles.title: RingCentral adds Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp to AI Receptionist
articles.summary: RingCentral expanded its AI Receptionist with integrations for Shopify, Calendly, and WhatsApp, enabling it to handle order inquiries, schedule appointments, and manage messages across multiple channels for over 11,800 businesses.

articles.title: AI helping ease the UK’s NHS burden
articles.summary: AI-enabled virtual care is being deployed in the UK NHS to address mounting pressures, including a 7.25 million patient waiting list, by managing care outside hospitals and reducing strain on hospital capacity.

articles.title: HP and the art of AI and data for the enterprise
articles.summary: HP discusses enterprise AI challenges, focusing on data preparation and processing for AI ingestion. The article explores whether organizations should use cloud-hosted or local compute solutions for AI models.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance developed Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses challenges in complex indoor environments by answering where robots are, where they're going, and how to get there.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to process unprecedented 100 million token context windows while maintaining accuracy, significantly expanding LLM capabilities.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and other AI coding assistants inadvertently leak sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries, posing security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, creating a significant supply chain vulnerability.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation to Recover Hidden Malware IOCs with FLARE-FLOSS Beyond Classic Strings Analysis
articles.summary: FLARE-FLOSS is a tool that recovers hidden and obfuscated strings from malware by using static analysis and emulation, uncovering IOCs that traditional string utilities miss.

articles.title: NVIDIA AI Releases Star Elastic: One Checkpoint that Contains 30B, 23B, and 12B Reasoning Models with Zero-Shot Slicing
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Star Elastic embeds multiple nested LLM variants in a single checkpoint, enabling 30B, 23B, and 12B models from one training run. This approach reduces compute costs and storage requirements for deploying multiple model sizes.

articles.title: 9 Best AI Tools for Spec-Driven Development in 2026: Kiro, BMAD, GSD, and More Compare
articles.summary: Spec-driven development addresses AI coding's speed-without-clarity problem by treating structured specifications as source of truth. The article reviews 9 AI tools like AWS Kiro that implement SDD workflows in 2026.

articles.title: My Roku apps were running slow - I considered 9 quick fixes before blaming my Wi-Fi
articles.summary: Roku TV apps running slow may not be due to Wi-Fi issues. The article explores nine troubleshooting fixes to improve performance before blaming internet connection.

articles.title: The best 85-inch TVs in 2026: Expert recommended
articles.summary: Expert review of top 85-inch TVs from Samsung, Sony and other brands for creating a cinematic home theater experience.

articles.title: Samsung watches can predict if you're about to faint - but there are big caveats
articles.summary: Samsung watches can predict fainting episodes, potentially helping the 40% of people who experience them. However, significant limitations and caveats exist regarding accuracy and reliability.

articles.title: Implementing Permission-Gated Tool Calling in Python Agents
articles.summary: AI agents now implement permission-gated tool calling in Python, enabling controlled access to functions and resources. This evolution moves agents beyond simple chatbots toward more secure, autonomous systems.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Tool Calling in AI Agents
articles.summary: Article outlines strategies for mastering tool calling capabilities in AI agents, enabling them to effectively utilize external functions and APIs.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: Non-deterministic agents produce varying outputs from identical inputs across multiple runs. Statistical guardrails help ensure reliable and consistent behavior despite this inherent variability.

articles.title: The Must-Know Topics for an LLM Engineer
articles.summary: Essential topics for LLM engineers span from tokenization to evaluation, covering how modern language models function in practice.

articles.title: RAG Is Blind to Time — I Built a Temporal Layer to Fix It in Production
articles.summary: RAG systems lack temporal awareness, retrieving outdated information instead of current facts. A temporal layer was built to filter expired data and prioritize time-sensitive information in production.

articles.title: From Data Scientist to AI Architect
articles.summary: The article discusses a shift in data science from model-centric thinking to a broader AI architecture approach, representing an evolution in how data scientists are developing into AI architects.

articles.title: Powering the Next American Century: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA’s Ian Buck on the Genesis Mission
articles.summary: US Energy Secretary Chris Wright and NVIDIA's Ian Buck discussed how AI requires massive energy resources, arguing American AI leadership depends on energy leadership and affordable power infrastructure.

articles.title: Linked and Loaded: Gaijin Single Sign-On Now Available on GeForce NOW
articles.summary: GeForce NOW now supports Gaijin single sign-on, enabling faster logins for cloud gaming. Seven new games have been added to the platform this week.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet is an advanced AI networking technology designed for large-scale AI infrastructure, deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle to support gigascale AI operations.

articles.title: Halliburton enhances seismic workflow creation with Amazon Bedrock and Generative AI
articles.summary: Halliburton partnered with AWS to develop an AI-powered assistant using Amazon Bedrock that simplifies seismic workflow creation for energy exploration, reducing manual configuration of complex processing tools.

articles.title: Secure short-term GPU capacity for ML workloads with EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans
articles.summary: AWS launches EC2 Capacity Blocks for ML and SageMaker training plans to address GPU scarcity, providing companies secure short-term access to GPU capacity for machine learning workloads.

articles.title: Overcoming reward signal challenges: Verifiable rewards-based reinforcement learning with GRPO on SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Training LLMs with reinforcement learning faces reward signal reliability challenges. RLVR introduces verification and transparency to improve training performance for objectively verifiable tasks like math and code generation.

articles.title: Improving Bash Generation in Small Language Models with Grammar-Constrained Decoding
articles.summary: Researchers improve Bash command generation in small language models using grammar-constrained decoding, enabling AI agents to produce valid shell commands for file operations and system tasks.

articles.title: Streaming Tokens and Tools: Multi-Turn Agentic Harness Support in NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo adds streaming tokens and tools support for multi-turn agentic interactions, enabling structured exchanges where assistants reason and call tools while users provide results.

articles.title: Achieving Peak System and Workload Efficiency on NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 with Slurm Block Scheduling
articles.summary: NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 extends NVLink coherence across entire racks, enabling exascale performance and requiring updated scheduling systems like Slurm to optimize rack-scale locality for peak efficiency.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study examining how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing distinct generational attitudes toward advertising.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030 and develops carbon mitigation technology to reduce its environmental footprint while sharing innovations openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to innovation in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning: The Next Paradigm in Efficient Inference Scaling
articles.summary: Adaptive Parallel Reasoning enables AI models to autonomously decide when to decompose tasks into parallel subtasks and coordinate them efficiently. This paradigm advances inference scaling by allowing models to dynamically adjust concurrency based on problem requirements.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, built-in stochasticity for exploration, and improved gradient shaping for visual dynamics.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. This work helps understand LLM decision-making processes through feature attribution analysis for safer AI systems.
